author: @abdelkadernamiq3894
text:
Nice job,

Will it be possible to make a video explaining how did you create this animation? and will will it be possible to connect some resistive load on the other coils.

As fare as I'm aware of FEMM does not support Back EMF and you can only fix the current, how did you managed to pulse the current at a fixed voltage?

Keep up the good work!

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Sorry about the error - just me being lazy in checking my typing - it actually ran on 150 milli amps at 6 volts so about 1 Watt

author: @bogy0101
text: If I good understood, your primary winding use xx volts and 150-250A, it is near 250W ???

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: I still find the system interesting so i continue to work on it. Flux density within the armature is obviously an issue - and, oddly enough - so is rate of change. The faster the system operates the worse the output. I am in the process of testing two more devices i have built and i will report quite soon when i have firmed up my results - i would be interested to see what you have been working on - good luck with it

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Hi, Thanks for your interesting comments. I looked at the Thane project and i can see what you mean about the similarities but i am more inclined to believe that the work is  more similar to what went on in US patent 3,368,141. So far I have had no real evidence of OU - I am beginning to think the amount of energy required to divert the flux is proportional to the amount of flux diverted, which in turn is proportional to the current at the coils less losses in the system.

author: @1HayesEng
text:
I like Your research.
This is similar to the "Thane Heins Ottawa University Bitt Transformer". They claim 400 to 500% O.U. This is done with flux diversion to stop Counter EMF from getting back to the primary coil reducing P.F. and heat due to out of phase head butting of the flux currents.

author: @1HayesEng
text:
Have You experienced any over-unity to date?
I am working on replicating one my self.
So far, I have no O.U.
